registration.description: Voter registration in India is the process of getting your name included in the Electoral Roll.
methods: Offline: Through your local Booth Level Officer (BLO)
requirements: Must be 18+ years old on the qualifying date
Must be an ordinary resident of the constituency

forms.Form 6A: Inclusion of name for Overseas (NRI) Electors
forms.Form 7: Objection to inclusion or deletion of name
forms.Form 8: Correction of entries, shifting of residence, or replacement of EPIC

timeline.description: The ECI updates and publishes the final list of eligible voters before the elections.
The official announcement of the election schedule, including dates for various voting phases.
Candidates file their nomination papers (Parchcha) with the Returning Officer.
Political parties hold rallies and public meetings. This strictly ends 48 hours before the close of polls (the Silence Period).
Voters visit assigned booths and cast their ballots using Electronic Voting Machines (EVMs).
EVMs are unsealed under heavy security, votes are tallied, and the final results (Nateeja) are declared.

voting.description: Casting your vote securely at your assigned polling booth.
voting.in_person: The primary method for all citizens using Electronic Voting Machines (EVM).
voting.postal_ballot: A special facility available only for 80+ senior citizens, Persons with Disabilities (PwD), and essential service/armed forces voters.
voting.vvpat: Voter Verifiable Paper Audit Trail. After pressing the EVM button, a printed slip showing your candidate's symbol is visible behind a glass window for 7 seconds to verify your vote.

documents.description: Valid identification required to cast your vote at the polling booth.
documents.primary: EPIC (Electors Photo Identity Card) or the digital e-EPIC.
alternatives: Passbook with photograph issued by Bank/Post Office
Smart Card issued by RGI under NPR
Health Insurance Smart Card issued under the scheme of Ministry of Labour
